skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Nano Banana 2 (Gemini-family flash-tier text-to-image) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents Nano Banana 2's strengths (rapid iteration, in-image typography rendering, predictable framing, optional web-grounded context), the resolution-tier pricing, the safety-tolerance dial, and when to route to Nano Banana Pro / GPT Image 2 / Flux 2 / Seedream instead. Calls `runcomfy run google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "nano banana", "nano-banana-2", "nano banana 2", "google image gen", "gemini image", or any explicit ask to generate with this model.
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Nano Banana 2 (Gemini-family flash-tier text-to-image) on RunComfy — bundled with optimized prompting patterns for sharp output.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation wrapper for the RunComfy CLI image generation command. All static findings are false positives. The 70 "external_commands" flags are code examples in markdown documentation showing terminal commands. No actual shell execution occurs. Network and filesystem flags reference legitimate RunComfy service URLs and standard token storage paths documented for users. The skill includes proper security mitigations including HTTPS-only API transmission and input boundary protection.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives — Documentation of Commands
low_findings.description: All 70 'external_commands' flags are false positives. These represent code blocks in markdown documentation showing terminal commands that users should execute, not actual Ruby/shell executions. The skill is a documentation wrapper with no runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains only markdown documentation. All code blocks show CLI commands as examples for users to execute via their terminal, not via code execution within the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: Network flags reference documented RunComfy service URLs. These are legitimate API endpoints for image generation, not suspicious external connections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to model-api.runcomfy.net and runcomfy.com domains which are the official service endpoints documented in the skill.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Path References Describe Standard Token Storage
low_findings.description: The ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json reference describes standard RunComfy CLI token storage location. This is documentation, not an actual filesystem operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Path documentation describes standard CLI token storage patterns, not malicious filesystem access.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Google Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Creating social thumbnails and marketing images requires rapid iteration and predictable output. This skill wraps the RunComfy CLI to generate images with Google Nano Banana 2, including optimized prompting patterns for sharper results than naive approaches.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using Google Nano Banana 2 via RunComfy CLI
Create batch variants (up to 4 images per request) for ideation
Render in-image typography with quoted text specifications
Apply web-grounding for current events and real entities
Control aspect ratio, resolution, and seed for reproducible outputs
Choose between flash-tier speed or Pro-tier quality based on use case
limitations: Still images only — no video generation on this endpoint
Higher resolutions (2K/4K) cost more than default 1K
Image editing requires a separate endpoint (nano-banana-edit skill)

use_cases.title: Social Media Thumbnails
use_cases.description: Generate multiple 9:16 or 4:5 aspect ratio variants quickly for A/B testing marketing campaigns. Use seed locking to refine a winning concept.

use_cases.title: Brand Asset Cards with Text
use_cases.description: Create product cards and promotional materials with precise typography rendering. Quote exact text strings for predictable in-image text.

use_cases.title: Rapid ideation drafts
use_cases.description: Generate low-resolution concept images fast at 0.5K, then promote the best to 2K final quality. Ideal for early-stage creative exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your subject, then environment, then style] for [use case].

prompt_templates.title: Portrait with typography
prompt_templates.prompt: A [adjective] portrait of [subject] [in/with action/environment], the label reads '[exact text]' in [font style] [position], [additional style details].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - for branded content with text

prompt_templates.title: Batch ideation round
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 variants of [concept description] at 0.5K resolution with seed [number] for [platform] aspect ratio, then we will pick the best for upscaling.

prompt_templates.title: Web-grounded current event
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of [concept referencing current event] with enable_web_search: true. Style: [art direction]. Include the headline text '[exact quote]' in [font].

output_examples.input: Generate a minimalist product card with the text 'Brewed Quietly' in clean bold sans-serif centered on a matte black ceramic mug
output_examples.output: A 4:3 product card image showing a matte black ceramic mug centered on soft warm-grey paper, with 'Brewed Quietly' rendered in clean bold white sans-serif text at the top, rim highlight from upper-left, balanced negative space below, e-commerce ready

output_examples.input: Create 4 vertical wellness brand thumbnails with 'Slow Down' hand-drawn serif text at the top
output_examples.output: A 4-up grid of 9:16 vertical images, each showing a ceramic teacup on a linen runner with soft morning side-light, the phrase 'Slow Down' in hand-drawn serif at the top, gentle steam rising, neutral color palette, uncluttered composition

output_examples.input: Cinematic portrait of a woman under neon lights in rainy Tokyo
output_examples.output: A cinematic close-up portrait of an American woman standing under neon lights in rainy Tokyo, shallow depth of field, reflective wet streets, ultra-detailed realistic skin texture, moody atmospheric lighting

best_practices: Front-load the primary subject in prompts, then trail with directives for action, environment, style, and camera settings
Quote exact text strings for in-image typography to ensure predictable rendering instead of hoping the model infers text
Lock the seed value when iterating on small prompt variants to maintain compositional stability across generations
anti_patterns: Avoid combining conflicting styles like 'minimalist + ornate + retro + cyberpunk' which cancel each other out — pick 1-2 anchors maximum
Do not request resolutions outside the 4 supported tiers (0.5K, 1K, 2K, 4K) or unsupported aspect ratios to avoid 422 errors
Avoid trying to verbally describe stable subject identity for editing — use the image-edit endpoint with reference images instead

faq.question: How does this differ from Nano Banana Pro?
faq.answer: Nano Banana 2 is the flash-tier model optimized for speed and iteration. Nano Banana Pro delivers higher quality for hyperrealistic portraits. Default to Pro for portraits, 2 for everything else.

faq.question: What resolution should I use?
faq.answer: Use 0.5K for rapid ideation batches, 1K as the default for general use, and 2K or 4K only for final outputs since higher resolutions cost more.

faq.question: How does in-image typography work?
faq.answer: Quote the exact characters you want in the image and specify placement and font style. Do not rely on vague descriptions like 'with the brand name on it'.

faq.question: What is web-grounding and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Web-grounding enables the model to use current information from the web. Enable it only for prompts referencing real-world events or entities, as it adds latency and cost.

faq.question: How do I get consistent results across generations?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter to lock reproducibility. When you find a prompt that works well, set a specific seed number to recreate that composition.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses the text-to-image endpoint only. For image editing (preserving subject while changing background or applying changes), use the separate nano-banana-edit skill.
